items.text: Notice: Parking Lot D will be closed for repaving from April 8 through April 10. Vehicles must be moved by 7:00 AM on April 8 or a $25 removal fee will apply. Drivers may park in Lot G near Wilson Hall during the closure at no extra charge.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what should drivers do during the repaving?

options.A: Park in Lot D and pay a daily fee.
options.B: Park along the curb of Wilson Hall instead.
options.C: Park in Lot G at no additional cost.
options.D: Park off campus and walk in for the week.

questions.explanation: The notice states that drivers may park in Lot G near Wilson Hall at no extra charge during the closure.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a car still parked in Lot D after 7:00 AM on April 8?

options.A: It is likely to receive a written warning.
options.B: It is likely to be ticketed for a moving violation.
options.C: It is likely to incur a $25 removal fee.
options.D: It is likely to be returned by lot staff later.

questions.explanation: The notice states that a $25 removal fee will apply to vehicles not moved by 7:00 AM on April 8.

items.text:
Hi Daniel,

Your replacement student ID is ready for pickup at the One-Card Office in Foster Hall, Room 105. Please bring a $10 fee in cash or card and a current photo ID. The office is open Monday through Friday until 4:00 PM.

Thanks,
One-Card Office

questions.stem: According to the email, where should Daniel go to pick up his ID?

options.A: Room 102 of the One-Card Office on campus.
options.B: Room 110 of the One-Card Office on campus.
options.C: Room 205 of the One-Card Office on campus.
options.D: Room 105 of the One-Card Office on campus.

questions.explanation: The email tells Daniel that the office is in Foster Hall, Room 105.

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Daniel bring to pick up the ID?

options.A: A $10 fee and a current photo ID.
options.B: A $5 fee and his class schedule.
options.C: A receipt from the housing office only.
options.D: A check made out to the cashier office.

questions.explanation: The email says Daniel must bring a $10 fee and a current photo ID.

items.text:
Hi Maria,

This is just a quick note to confirm your statistics tutoring session on Thursday at 3:00 PM in Carlton Hall, Room 218. Please bring your most recent problem set so we can review it together.

See you then,
Jordan

questions.stem: According to the email, where will the tutoring session take place?

options.A: In Carlton Hall, Room 118 on Thursday.
options.B: In Carlton Hall, Room 218 on Thursday.
options.C: In Foster Hall, Room 218 on Thursday.
options.D: In Carlton Hall, Room 318 on Thursday.

questions.explanation: The email confirms the session in Carlton Hall, Room 218.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the focus of the upcoming session?

options.A: It will center on Maria's most recent problem set.
options.B: It will introduce a new statistics textbook chapter.
options.C: It will be a recorded review of a missed lecture.
options.D: It will replace Maria's regular class meeting that week.

questions.explanation: Jordan asks Maria to bring her most recent problem set so they can review it together.

items.text:
Hi advisees,

My office hours this week are moved to Wednesday from 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM in Beech Hall, Room 312. Bring the $15 fee for the spring transcript review if you have not paid yet. Email me if that time does not work.

Dr. Lee

questions.stem: According to the email, when are this week's office hours?

options.A: On Tuesday from 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM.
options.B: On Wednesday from 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM.
options.C: On Thursday from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM.
options.D: On Wednesday from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM.

questions.explanation: Dr. Lee states that office hours this week are on Wednesday from 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about advisees who cannot attend at this time?

options.A: They should attend Dr. Lee's lecture instead this week.
options.B: They are likely to lose their advising spot for the semester.
options.C: They must reschedule through the registrar's office portal.
options.D: They are encouraged to contact Dr. Lee by email.

questions.explanation: Dr. Lee asks students to email him if that time does not work.
